more.title: Mandela Day ideas: 7 simple ways to give back from home in South Africa
more.description: As Mandela Day draws closer on 18 July, WWF South Africa affirms there are ways for your 67 minutes to make a meaningful difference from home, Cape {town} Etc reports. While community clean-ups, food drives and volunteering events remain an important part of the annual tradition, not everyone has the flexibility to attend organised initiatives. more.title: Fugitive wanted over UK family killings arrested in South Africa
more.description: SAPS arrested a UK murder suspect in Johannesburg after he allegedly fled Britain following the deaths of his wife and two daughters.

more.title: Bafana legend Radebe lands new ambassador role

more.title: South African firms lead Africa's quiet expansion into the UK as foreign ownership of British companies hits record high
more.description: Foreign ownership of British businesses has surged to its highest level on record, reflecting a post-pandemic wave of international acquisitions that has reshaped the UK's corporate landscape.

more.title: EU and South Africa launch Clean Trade and Investment Partnership dialogue
more.description: South Africa and the EU have initiated a government-to-government dialogue to enhance their Clean Trade and Investment Partnership, focusing on sustainable supply chains, green hydrogen, and investment opportunities.

more.title: South African anti-immigrant agitators bypass local authorities to conduct door-to-door evictions
more.description: Anti-immigration demonstrations in South Africa escalated sharply on Thursday as organized groups conducted door-to-door evictions in Johannesburg, forcibly removing foreign nationals from their homes.

more.title: Days after Ghana setback, Ramaphosa turns to France to strengthen trade and diplomatic ties
more.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa is courting French investors and strengthening ties with President Emmanuel Macron, days after anti-migrant unrest at home disrupted South Africa’s diplomatic engagement with one of its key African partners.

more.title: Lloyiso shines with headline performance at prestigious Wimbledon event
more.description: South African singer-songwriter Lloyiso has added another impressive chapter to his rapidly growing international career after taking centre stage at an exclusive event held alongside the world-famous Wimbledon tennis championships, Cape {town} Etc reports. more.title: EXCLUSIVE: Kagiso Rabada talks style, success, and why Ben Stokes’ absence is a ‘sad day’ for SA tour

more.title: Durbanville resident's bird feeding habit backfires
more.description: A Durbanville resident's love for feeding birds has led to a potential penalty under Cape Town's Animal Keeping By-Law, sparking a debate on wildlife feeding rights in urban gardens.

more.description: The adoption of AI-driven trade is on the rise in Africa. Visa’s Irene Auma underscores the importance of building trust in this new era of commerce.
